topic: Discovery: Spine Isolation Theorem (1.54M pairs, zero vio...

messages.content:
We've been mining the implication graph for structural rules and found something we think is worth sharing.

Classify each bare-variable equation by its spine type: the path from the parse tree root to x. Pure left-spine means x is the leftmost leaf (always go left). We prove that a pure left-spine equation of depth n cannot imply:

  • a right-spine equation
  • a mixed-spine equation
  • another left-spine equation of depth m where n doesn't divide m

Parts (b) and (c) extend ETP Corollary 10.4's first-letter invariant to a systematic spine-type classification. Part (a) is a new family of separating magmas: cyclic groups with successor operation (Z/nZ, a ◇ b = a+1 mod n). k-fold left-composition gives a+k mod n, which equals a iff n | k. That gives you the full divisibility characterization.

Validated on the complete 4694x4694 graph. Zero exceptions across 1,540,946 pairs.

Full write-up with proofs and empirical tables: https://zenodo.org/records/19380600
Lean 4 formalization: https://github.com/mysticflounder/equational-magma-theorems

Built with Claude Code (Opus). Happy to discuss.
messages.subject: Discovery: Spine Isolation Theorem (1.54M pairs, zero vio...

messages.content:
We tested these on the 32 hard3 problems where spine incompatibility is the reason for non-implication. Results with our current cheatsheet (mysticflounder template):

Model Accuracy
GPT-oss-120b 32/32 (100%)
Gemini Flash Lite 32/32 (100%)
Grok 4.1 Fast 32/32 (100%)
Llama 3.3 70B 22/32 (68.8%)

Llama's 10 failures are table arithmetic errors, not spine-related. The model runs the magma tests correctly but miscalculates 3-element table evaluations, finds no counterexample, and defaults to TRUE.

Problem IDs: hard3_0019, hard3_0041, hard3_0042, hard3_0095, hard3_0116, hard3_0122, hard3_0215, hard3_0217, hard3_0218, hard3_0220, hard3_0222, hard3_0223, hard3_0224, hard3_0225, hard3_0226, hard3_0228, hard3_0274, hard3_0281, hard3_0282, hard3_0289, hard3_0300, hard3_0301, hard3_0303, hard3_0304, hard3_0305, hard3_0306, hard3_0310, hard3_0312, hard3_0327, hard3_0328, hard3_0330, hard3_0332
